tasks.title: Memoize TaskRow + CommentItem + ActivityFeed + cache parsed JSON in KanbanBoard/GanttChart
tasks.description:
Fix the most impactful frontend performance issues — component memoization and JSON parse caching:

**1. Memoize TaskRow** — In `apps/web/src/components/SprintSection.tsx`, wrap the `TaskRow` component export with `React.memo()`. TaskRow currently re-renders on every parent state change (drag, selection, checkbox toggle) even when its own props haven't changed. The `orgUsers.find()` call at line 53 is an O(n) lookup on every render — move it into a `useMemo` inside TaskRow. Ensure the memo comparison works by checking that parent components (`BacklogView.tsx`) use stable references for callback props (wrap with `useCallback` where needed).

**2. Memoize CommentItem** — In `apps/web/src/components/CommentSection.tsx`, wrap the `CommentItem` function component (lines 27-105) with `React.memo()`. In the parent rendering section (lines 226-268), wrap the `onReply`, `onUpdate`, `onDelete` callbacks with `useCallback` so they don't break the memo.

**3. Memoize ActivityFeed items** — In `apps/web/src/components/ActivityFeed.tsx`, extract the activity item rendering (lines 69-80) into a separate `ActivityItem` component wrapped in `React.memo()`. The `describeActivity()` and `timeAgo()` calls are pure functions of the activity object, so memo will prevent re-computation.

**4. Cache parsed JSON** — In `apps/web/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx` (line 55), `JSON.parse(task.dependsOn)` is called inside a `useMemo` for every task. Create a utility function `parseDependsOn(raw: string | null): string[]` in `apps/web/src/utils/taskHelpers.ts` that caches the parsed result using a WeakMap or simple Map keyed by the raw string. Use this utility in KanbanBoard (line 55) and GanttChart (line 187) instead of inline `JSON.parse`. Also create `parseColumns(raw: string): string[]` for the 6 Sprint.columns parse sites — this is shared with W2's JSON helpers task but the utility file location doesn't overlap (taskHelpers.ts vs the actual component files).
acceptance: TaskRow is wrapped in React.memo with stable callback props
CommentItem is wrapped in React.memo with useCallback'd parent callbacks
ActivityFeed items extracted as memoized ActivityItem component
parseDependsOn utility created in taskHelpers.ts with caching
KanbanBoard and GanttChart use parseDependsOn instead of inline JSON.parse
No functional regressions — drag-and-drop, selection, keyboard nav all still work
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: Lazy-load route components + optimize portfolioOverview N+1 queries
tasks.description:
Two independent performance improvements — route code-splitting and API query optimization:

**1. Route lazy-loading** — In `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, convert the heaviest page imports to `React.lazy()`: `ProjectDetail` (346 lines), `Portfolio` (imports GanttChart at 436 lines), `OrgSettings`, `Search`. Keep small pages (Login, Signup, CreateOrg, Home) as eager imports since they're on the critical auth path. The existing `<Suspense>` wrapper (line 33) already exists — just ensure the fallback shows the existing loading spinner. Example: `const ProjectDetail = lazy(() => import('./pages/ProjectDetail'));`. Also lazy-load the heavy modal components that are rarely used: `GanttChart`, `BatchCodeGenModal`, `DriftAnalysisModal` — these are imported in ProjectDetail but only rendered when their specific view/modal is active.

**2. Optimize portfolioOverview resolver** — In `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/project.ts` (lines 30-94), the `portfolioOverview` query does 1 + 2N database queries (fetch projects, then per-project: fetch tasks + fetch active sprint). Optimize to 3 total queries: (a) fetch all projects, (b) fetch ALL tasks for those projects in one query with `where: { projectId: { in: projectIds } }`, then group by projectId in JavaScript, (c) fetch ALL active sprints for those projects in one query with `where: { projectId: { in: projectIds }, isActive: true, closedAt: null }`. This reduces 11 queries (5 projects) to 3 queries.
acceptance: ProjectDetail, Portfolio, OrgSettings, Search are lazy-loaded with React.lazy
Existing Suspense fallback shows loading state during chunk load
Build output shows separate chunks for lazy-loaded routes
portfolioOverview uses at most 3 queries regardless of project count
Portfolio page still renders correctly with all metrics
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: Task templates — Prisma model, GraphQL CRUD, create-from-template UI
tasks.description:
Full vertical slice for reusable task templates:

**1. Prisma model** — Create `apps/api/prisma/schema/template.prisma` with a `TaskTemplate` model: `id` (cuid), `orgId` (FK to Org), `projectId` (FK to Project, nullable — null means org-wide template), `name` (String), `description` (String?), `instructions` (String?), `acceptanceCriteria` (String?), `priority` (String, default 'medium'), `taskType` (String, default 'task'), `estimatedHours` (Float?), `storyPoints` (Int?), `createdAt` (DateTime). Add indexes on `[orgId]` and `[projectId]`. Add the reverse relation on Org and Project models (in `auth.prisma` and `project.prisma`). Run `npx prisma generate` and `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-task-templates`.

**2. GraphQL types + resolvers** — Add to `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/template.ts`: `TaskTemplate` type, `taskTemplates(projectId: ID)` query (returns org-wide + project templates), `createTaskTemplate(...)` mutation, `updateTaskTemplate(...)` mutation, `deleteTaskTemplate(id)` mutation, `createTaskFromTemplate(templateId: ID!, projectId: ID!, title: String!)` mutation. Add resolvers in `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/template.ts`. The `createTaskFromTemplate` mutation should create a new Task pre-filled with the template's fields, overriding with the provided title. Register in `resolvers/index.ts` and `typedefs/index` (or schema.ts). All require org membership.

**3. Frontend UI** — In `apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx`, add a "Template" button in the toolbar actions area (near the + Add task button). Clicking it opens a dropdown/modal showing available templates for this project. Selecting a template opens a simple form pre-filled with the template's fields, letting the user modify the title before creating. Add a "Save as Template" option in the task detail panel (`TaskDetailPanel.tsx` or its toolbar) that creates a template from the current task's fields. Add template management (list/edit/delete) to `ProjectSettingsModal.tsx` as a new tab.
acceptance: TaskTemplate model exists with migration applied
Run npx prisma generate && pnpm typecheck passes
taskTemplates query returns org-wide + project-specific templates
createTaskFromTemplate creates a task pre-filled with template fields
ProjectDetail toolbar has Template button with template picker
Save as Template option exists on task detail
Template management tab in ProjectSettingsModal (list/edit/delete)
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: JSON string column helpers + custom field reorder UI in ProjectSettingsModal
tasks.description:
Centralize scattered JSON.parse calls and add the missing custom field reorder UI:

**1. JSON string column helpers** — Create centralized parsing utilities in `apps/web/src/utils/jsonHelpers.ts` (new file): `parseColumns(raw: string | null): string[]` (with fallback to `['todo', 'in_progress', 'done']`), `parseOptions(raw: string | null): string[]` (with fallback to `[]`), `parseStatuses(raw: string | null): string[]` (with fallback to `['todo', 'in_progress', 'done']`). Each should have a try-catch returning the fallback on parse failure. Then replace all 6 `JSON.parse(*.columns)` calls across: `ProjectDetail.tsx:701`, `SprintCreateModal.tsx:22`, `CloseSprintModal.tsx:32`, `useTaskCRUD.ts:132,210,305`. Replace all 3 `JSON.parse(*.options)` calls in: `TaskCustomFieldsSection.tsx:69`, `ProjectSettingsModal.tsx:321`, `FilterBar.tsx:99`. Replace the `JSON.parse(*.statuses)` call in `useProjectData.ts:157`. Import from the new helpers file.

**2. Custom field reorder UI** — In `apps/web/src/components/ProjectSettingsModal.tsx`, in the Custom Fields tab (around lines 310-379), add Up/Down arrow buttons next to each custom field in the list. Clicking Up swaps the field with the one above it (lower position number); Down swaps with below. Each swap calls the existing `updateCustomField` mutation with the new `position` value for both affected fields. Show the fields sorted by position. Disable Up on the first field and Down on the last. Use simple arrow buttons (▲/▼) — no drag-and-drop needed.
acceptance: jsonHelpers.ts exists with parseColumns, parseOptions, parseStatuses functions
All 6 Sprint.columns JSON.parse calls replaced with parseColumns()
All 3 CustomField.options JSON.parse calls replaced with parseOptions()
Project.statuses JSON.parse call replaced with parseStatuses()
Custom fields in ProjectSettingsModal show Up/Down arrow buttons
Clicking Up/Down swaps position with adjacent field via updateCustomField mutation
Fields render sorted by position
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass

tasks.title: CLAUDE.md refresh + knowledge base audit + update decisions.md
tasks.description:
Update project documentation to reflect current state after 11 waves of development:

**1. CLAUDE.md refresh** — Read the current `CLAUDE.md` and update it to reflect the full current state:
- **Commands section:** Add `pnpm test` (runs Vitest across both packages). Add `pnpm dev` as the recommended dev command.
- **GraphQL Schema section:** Add missing queries: `taskTemplates`, `slackUserMappings`, `sprintVelocity`, `sprintBurndown`, `portfolioOverview`, `globalSearch`, `projectStats`, `unreadNotificationCount`. Add missing mutations: `generateProjectOptions`, `createProjectFromOption`, `previewTaskPlan`, `commitTaskPlan`, `addTaskAssignee`, `removeTaskAssignee`, `mapSlackUser`, `unmapSlackUser`, `createTaskTemplate`, `createTaskFromTemplate`. Note the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header requirement.
- **Key Files section:** Add new key files: `apps/api/src/utils/metrics.ts` (Prometheus), `apps/api/src/utils/logger.ts` (pino), `apps/api/src/graphql/loaders.ts` (DataLoaders), `apps/web/src/utils/jsonHelpers.ts` (JSON column parsers), `apps/web/vitest.config.ts`, `apps/api/vitest.config.ts`.
- **Architecture section:** Add notes about: health check at `/api/health`, metrics at `/api/metrics`, Prisma `metrics` preview feature, fetch-based SSE client (not native EventSource), multiple assignees via TaskAssignee join table.
- **Env Files section:** Add `LOG_LEVEL` and `SENTRY_DSN` (optional) to the API env vars list.

**2. Knowledge base audit** — Read `app-overview.md`, `skills.md`, and `decisions.md`. For each:
- `app-overview.md`: Verify the data model section includes TaskAssignee, TaskTemplate, SlackUserMapping, WebhookDelivery models. Update the file map if needed.
- `skills.md`: Add patterns for: Vitest test setup, prom-client metrics, pino-http logging, React.lazy code-splitting.
- `decisions.md`: Add decisions from Waves 9-11: SSE moved from EventSource to fetch-based client (security), Prisma metrics preview feature enabled, Vitest chosen over Jest (simpler config, faster), prom-client for metrics (industry standard).
acceptance: CLAUDE.md Commands section includes pnpm test
CLAUDE.md GraphQL Schema section lists all current queries and mutations
CLAUDE.md Key Files section includes metrics.ts, logger.ts, loaders.ts, vitest configs
CLAUDE.md Architecture section mentions /api/health, /api/metrics, fetch-based SSE
app-overview.md data model includes TaskAssignee, SlackUserMapping, WebhookDelivery
decisions.md includes SSE migration, Vitest choice, Prisma metrics decisions
skills.md includes Vitest, prom-client, pino-http patterns
No stale or contradictory information remains in any knowledge base file
tasks.reviewNotes: CLAUDE.md includes the Role Swarm Worker (worker-3) section at the end. This is worktree-specific and must NOT be committed to main. Remove everything after the Notes section that ends with Package manager is pnpm. The legitimate doc updates are all good - just drop the worker role block, re-commit, and mark completed.

tasks.title: Swarm status dashboard file overlap warnings + worker branch strategy docs
tasks.description:
Improve swarm tooling based on 11 waves of process issues:

**1. Swarm status file overlap warnings** — In `scripts/swarm/status.sh`, after displaying the current task summary, add a section that detects potential merge conflicts. For each pair of in-progress or completed (not yet merged) tasks across different workers, compare their `files` arrays. If any files overlap, print a warning: `⚠ OVERLAP: task-001 (worker-1) and task-005 (worker-3) both touch: apps/api/src/utils/sseManager.ts`. Use `jq` or `node` to parse tasks.json and compute file intersections. This helps the reviewer anticipate merge conflicts before they happen.

**2. Worker branch strategy documentation** — Create `.claude/skills/swarm-worker/BRANCH_STRATEGY.md` documenting the recommended git workflow for workers:
- Workers should commit after each task completion (not batch all tasks into one commit)
- If a worker's first task is merged and they have a second task on the same branch, they should `git rebase main` before starting the second task to incorporate the merge
- If a worker's first task is rejected, fix it on the same branch before starting the next task
- Workers should NOT create separate branches per task (the worktree IS the branch)
- Document the `scripts/swarm/merge-worker.sh` behavior: it squash-merges into main, runs prisma generate if schema changed, validates typecheck/lint/build

**3. Task description standard notes** — Update `.claude/skills/swarm/SKILL.md` to include standard boilerplate that gets auto-added to task descriptions based on content: (a) if the task mentions adding a Prisma model, auto-add to acceptance criteria: 'Run npx prisma generate && pnpm typecheck passes'. (b) if the task mentions installing a new npm package, auto-add: 'Verify pnpm typecheck passes — add @types/* or .d.ts if needed'. Add these as reminders in the Planning section of the skill.
acceptance: status.sh shows file overlap warnings between in-flight tasks across workers
Overlap detection works with jq or node parsing of tasks.json files arrays
BRANCH_STRATEGY.md documents commit-per-task, rebase-after-merge, rejection handling
swarm/SKILL.md includes standard Prisma and npm package acceptance criteria reminders
All scripts are executable and work correctly
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build all pass (no code changes, but verify)
